speaker: Narrator
line: Something interesting came out of this week's sequencing run for request 50004-000001, but unfortunately, it is not the kind of discovery we want to see in a clinical pipeline.

speaker: Narrator
line: We have identified a systematic failure in MSH2 coverage, specifically a recurrent dropout at exon 8. This is not a stochastic sample failure; it is a clear panel design flaw.

speaker: Narrator
line: This locus at Chromosome 2, position 47442251, is critical for Lynch syndrome screening. Currently, the depth is insufficient for reliable variant calling, which directly compromises our diagnostic integrity.

speaker: Narrator
line: The enrichment metrics are highly inconsistent. We are seeing mean coverage depths plummeting to 20x, which is far below the clinical threshold required for high-confidence heterozygous variant detection.

speaker: Narrator
line: Furthermore, on-target mapping rates are reaching a floor of 10%. This indicates significant off-target capture and suboptimal hybridization kinetics during the enrichment step.

speaker: Narrator
line: With up to 150 low-coverage regions per sample, the analytical sensitivity of the assay is currently sub-threshold for clinical use.

speaker: Narrator
line: We must initiate an immediate probe redesign for the MSH2 locus. Until that is validated, implement a mandatory Sanger sequencing backfill for this region for all Lynch syndrome referrals.

speaker: Narrator
line: Additionally, we need to optimize our wash stringency parameters to improve capture specificity and increase the on-target mapping rate to reach a 100x mean depth minimum.

speaker: Narrator
line: Let’s get these protocol adjustments into validation immediately to ensure we maintain clinical sensitivity. I will be in the dry lab if you need to review the coverage plots.
